interactions.body: Hi Darlene, we need your bank details to verify your identity and to deposit the $3000 into for you to give to Shane, to make sure you’re fully aware of the loan. Also, as you agree to make any payments Shane doesn’t, we use these as a backup payment method ONLY if they don’t pay. To continue your application, use this link https://tcrdt.com/s/7KYLTSOP or you can let me know if you need more help. Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

(Requesting lower loan without a GT SMS) Hi Shane, Together Loans requires a cosigner, regardless of the requested loan amount. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you and that we should too, regardless of your credit score. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a very good credit history with no trouble paying bills in the past, and who can afford to make the monthly payments if you don't. Although they don't have to be a homeowner, it may increase the possibility of us accepting them as your cosigner if they do own their own home. If you need to update your loan amount or term, you can do so here: https://tcrdt.com/s/46XHQGAX Thanks, Together Loans

(AI CB RED - Identity theft confusion Email) [RE: ] Hi Shane,

Thank you for your email.

I understand that you're concerned an open account with us is appearing on your credit report and you are worried that you may be a victim of identity fraud. I have investigated your concerns and am pleased to be able to confirm that you do not owe Together Loans any money and no funds have been paid out to anyone in your name. The account showing on your credit report is related to our Credit Building product that you opened with us, but don't worry, this does not mean you have any outstanding liability to us.

When you applied for a loan with us on 10/14/2025, we offered you two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder, which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, helping to build up their credit score. CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not, as part of their application process. I can see that when you reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, you chose to proceed with it.

After doing so, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Together Loans Builder!” email to teganfields@gmail.com, confirming your account and giving you access to login and manage it yourself. The email explained again that we would collect monthly payments of $5 a month from you which would be reported to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score! We also explained that if you decided to cancel or stop paying, CreditBuilder would simply be settled and marked as paid in full - preventing any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

We sent this to you on 10/14/2025, and we've also been sending you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment reminding you that your $5 payment was due to the same address since. It's probably worth you checking your spam folder in case the emails have been sent there but everything I can see points to an account that was opened intentionally by yourself, in line with the events above.

However, given the confusion that has occurred, I have proactively canceled your Credit Builder account assuming this is not something you wish to benefit from further. Your Credit Builder loan will also now be marked as paid and settled on your credit report. Once again, please rest assured that you do not and never have owed us any money under this Credit Builder loan.

I hope I have been able to resolve your concerns, however, should you have any further concerns or queries, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Many thanks,
Ben, Together Loans

Hi Shane, thank you for contacting Together Loans, your message is really important to us. We offer cosigner loans of $3,000 to $10,000 over 2 to 5 years, depending on how much you will borrow and the state you are located in. Our interest is calculated daily, so any additional payment could save you money in the long run. We recommend all of our customers pay as much as they can afford. You can make your payments before your selected due date and there is no cost for doing so. 
To amend your details and change your loan amount, you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/46XHQGAX
Thanks, Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0623092123012204090904] Hi Shane,

Thank you for taking the time to speak with us over the phone today. We really appreciate your time and the opportunity to discuss this with you.

I understand that you’re concerned an open account with us is appearing on your credit report and you are worried that you may be a victim of identity fraud. I have investigated your concerns and am pleased to be able to confirm that you do not owe Together Loans any money and no funds have been paid out to anyone in your name. The account showing on your credit report is related to our Credit Building product that you opened with us, but don’t worry, this does not mean you have any outstanding liability to us. 

When you applied for a loan with us on 10/14/2025, we offered you two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder, which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, helping to build up their credit score. CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not, as part of their application process. I can see that when you reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, you chose to proceed with it. 

After doing so, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Together Loans CreditBuilder!” email to teganfields@gmail.com, confirming your account and giving you access to login and manage it yourself. The email explained again that we would collect monthly payments of $5 a month from you which would be reported to the Credit Bureau as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score! We also explained that if you decided to cancel or stop paying, CreditBuilder would simply be settled and marked as paid in full - preventing any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score. 

We sent this to you on 10/14/2025, and we’ve also been sending you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment reminding you that your $5 payment was due, to the same address since. It’s probably worth you checking your spam folder in case the emails have been sent there but everything I can see points to an account that was opened intentionally by yourself, in line with the events above.

However, given the confusion that has occurred, I have proactively canceled your Credit Builder account assuming this is not something you wish to benefit from further. Your Credit Builder loan will also now be marked as paid and settled on your credit report. Once again, please rest assured that you do not and never have owed us any money under this Credit Builder loan.

We have also attached the Credit Builder Promissory Note with this email which you signed and accepted for your reference.

I hope I have been able to resolve your concerns, however, should you have any further concerns or queries, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Many thanks, 
Bushra@Together Loans

PS-Just to let you know, we have rebranded! Transform Credit is now Together Loans. We chose our new name because we're here to build something better. It's the same company, same team, and same loan application. You don't need to do anything, it's just a new name that better reflects what we stand for. Your application and personal information remain with Transform Credit Inc., which is now doing business as Together Loans. Find out more at https://togetherloans.com/faq/transform-credit-is-now-together-loans

They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/K9D3JVOB - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

interactions.body: [Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0605070907273423060633] I don’t have a co signer.  Where do I go from here?

(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0605070907273423060633] Hi Tamela, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/V3IOEBQB

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Francisco Munoz,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is funded. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be canceled. interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Carmesicia, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/9Q11SG65 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0632133109082430103304] Hi Amanda,

Thank you for contacting Transform Credit, your message is really important to us.

I can see that your application is active and you still have some tasks to finish before the loan is paid. Your current application contemplates a loan of $3250, over 24 months, paying back $191.87 monthly, but you can change these terms anytime before the loan is paid.

Transform Credit is a cosigner-based lender.  This means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. Normally, a cosigner is a friend or a family member who knows you (the borrower) and trusts that it is a good idea for you to borrow money. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a strong history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.

How does the application journey work? When you apply online, we will send you a unique link for your cosigner to register. As soon as we have everything we need from both of you, we’ll transfer the money. The money is transferred into your cosigner's account. This is to protect them, and us, against identity fraud, as transferring the money into their account ensures that they are fully aware of the loan.

You can check here more information about the application journey and common questions: https://transformcredit.com/faq 

To complete your application journey and check what is the next step, you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/UT0ZTRFA

Any other questions, please let me know.

Have a nice day,
Latoya@TransformCredit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Aimee Anspach,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Aimee, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/X5TYLTAH - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

interactions.label: Message from MessageFactoryBot — INVITE GT BRW Email DSIT5

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0634023127082210252606] Hi Aimee,

I understand you may feel uncomfortable or awkward asking a friend to help you, but we recommend asking them in person so they can ask you questions and you can have an open discussion about it.

We also recommend explaining to them why you need the loan - they may be more willing to help you out if they understand why you need this money and may feel good about helping you achieve something important.

It may also reassure them if you explain to them that you can afford your monthly repayments. If you really trust them, you could even go through your finances and budgeting together to show them that you are confident you can afford this.

Make sure to explain to them what being a cosigner means, so they can make an informed decision. They need to know that they will be making any payments that you cannot.

This is a big commitment, so please don't feel disheartened if the first person you asks says no. You may need to ask a few different people before you find the right cosigner.

You might find this page helpful: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans - you could also send it to a potential cosigner to help answer any of their questions. Please let us know if you need any further assistance or help.

Thanks, Transform Credit

interactions.body:
(AI CB RED - No refund Email) [RE: ] Hi Brandon,

Thank you for reaching out.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not sign up for the CreditBuilder loan, as part of the application process. I can see that on 11/25/2025, when you applied for this loan of $3000, you also reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, and you chose to sign up when clicked on “ok, set up my account”.

After signing up, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Together Loans CreditBuilder!” email, confirming your account, and further explaining the benefits of the product you had opted into. I can see this was sent to you at 11/25/2025 to cstgirl@msn.com. The email also provided you with your login link, so you could access your account and manage/cancel it, at any time, even before the first payment.

When you signed up for CreditBuilder, you took the first step in building your score. Each month you pay $5, we report these payments to the Credit Bureaus as interest against the loan that has been held on your behalf. These successful payments build your credit history, helping to improve your score. The great thing about CreditBuilder is that if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full and so missed payments can never undo the positive impact that your on-time payments have been making on your credit score. Our customers use CreditBuilder as a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment.

Since 11/25/2025, we've sent you monthly payment reminders to your email address above, reminding you that your $5 CreditBuilder payment was due. These reminders again provided you with your login details, where you were able to manage or cancel your CreditBuilder, should you have decided you no longer wanted to benefit from the product. We've also been reporting the CreditBuilder loan to Experian, demonstrating any payments you have made. As these on-time payments accumulate, they contribute positively to your overall credit score, paving the way for better credit opportunities in the future.

Based on the above, we will be unable to issue you a refund for the payments that you made during this period. You chose to actively benefit from the product during this time, despite us being open and honest in all of our messages about your CreditBuilder account; including giving you the opportunity to close this at any point in time.

However, your Credit Builder account is now canceled and will now be marked as paid and settled on your credit report. Once again, please rest assured that you do not and never have owed us any money under this Credit Builder loan.

Many thanks,
Ben, Together Loans

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0620290225121914220605] Hi Melissa,

Thank you for contacting Transform Credit, your message is really important to us.

During the loan application journey, once the cosigner application is filed and accepted, we will need to talk to the applicants via video call, but your cosigner can use someone else's device or ask someone for help with it - just remember that important information will be sent to the registered cell phone.

Please let us know if you need more help.

Thanks, 
Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Latisha, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tglns.com/s/5RDC68MJ - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0602140213290116081101] Hi Lillian,

Thank you for contacting Together Loans.

I can see that your application is active and you still have some tasks to finish before the loan is paid. Your current application contemplates a loan of $3000, over 36 months, paying back $127.35 monthly, but you can change these terms anytime before the loan is paid.

Together Loans is a cosigner-based lender.  This means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. Normally, a cosigner is a friend or a family member who knows you (the borrower) and trusts that it is a good idea for you to borrow money. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a strong history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.

How does the application journey work? When you apply online, we will send you a unique link for your cosigner to register. As soon as we have everything we need from both of you, we’ll transfer the money. The money is transferred into your cosigner's account. This is to protect them, and us, against identity fraud, as transferring the money into their account ensures that they are fully aware of the loan.

You can check here more information about the application journey and common questions: https://togetherloans.com/faq 

To complete your application journey and check what is the next step, you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/L5TIXBIE

Any other questions, please let me know.

Have a nice day,
Together Loans

interactions.body: (I need more time SMS) Hi Regina. Take as long as you need, we will keep your application open for you. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners are more likely to be accepted). Most of our customers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner because they know and trust you the most. When you find someone to be your cosigner, just give them this link so they can apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/MQ41MEAV . In the meantime, it might help you to check out this page: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans and if you have any more questions, please let us know. Thanks, Transform Credit

(AI BW Veriff - Need to do ID Email) [RE: Ezzard needs to finalize their agreement here: https://tcrdt.com/s/SUWJQHD4] Hi Regina,

Ezzard needs to upload their ID via the link we sent them to continue.

We do this to prevent identity fraud from being committed against them. Ezzard needs to upload their physical U.S. ID, that belongs to them and is in date. We accept driver's licenses, passports, ID cards and resident permits.

Ask them to add their ID here: https://tcrdt.com/s/SUWJQHD4 or you can add a new cosigner here: https://tcrdt.com/s/QX0WLLMB

Thanks,
Transform Credit

(Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Regina, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Kayden Whipple,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Amanda, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/A175M2DM - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

(AI BW GTQs - Don't have/won't provide GT SMS) Hi Amanda, Transform Credit is a cosigner-only lender, which means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. If you can't provide a suitable cosigner, we can cancel your application if you prefer. You can apply again anytime! You can reply CANCEL to this message, or use this link to continue when you are ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/5QOSQ8UB Thanks, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Vanessa, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0626032333083114040600] Dear Mario,

Thank you for contacting Transform Credit.
 
We can see here that you applied for a loan via the Transform Credit website and during the application process, signed up to our CreditBuilder Account. 

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us as a Borrower, it is completely optional, giving you the opportunity to opt-in, or continue the loan application without setting up a CreditBuilder Account.

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan which is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments undoing the positive impact that your on time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

How are these payments reported to the Credit Reporting Agencies? In order for us to be able to report the successful payments to the credit bureaus, we reflect a loan in the customer’s credit file, which is held in escrow by a corporate guarantor, hence a Credit Builder customer never receives the loan. When the Credit Builder Account is cancelled, this loan is then reported as successfully paid off and no money is owed to us from the customer.
 
You can find out more about the Transform Credit builder here: https://transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/

How to manage your CreditBuilder Account:
1. check the email you used to start your Credit Builder account here:  https://transformcredit.com/details?aRef=0626032333083114040600
2. click here to access your CreditBuilder account using your SSN and the email you registered:  www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login.
3. after your login, you will see at the bottom of the page the "account settings", this is where you can manage your account. If you click on "settings" again, you can choose to cancel or to keep your account. 

Alternatively, if you think you don’t need to improve your credit score further and want to cancel your Credit Builder account, please reply to this email with “Cancel CreditBuilder”, and one of our agents will close your account promptly to ensure no extra payments are collected. 

For any other information, or further support we will be happy to help you.
 
Kind regards,
Credit Builder Team
[Fw: Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0626032333083114040600] Don't need it

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0626032333083114040600] Hi Mario,
 
Thank you for your contact.

I have canceled your Credit builder account for you, and you will not be charged again.

If you have any other questions, just let us know.

Have a nice day,
Rebekah@TransformCredit

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. (Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Co-signer] Hi Bonita, 

Here at Together Loans, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://togetherloans.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/K4VIIWHB

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with CANCEL. You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://togetherloans.com

Thanks,
Together Loans

(AI GT Veriff - ID issues SMS) Hi Mariarlene, if you are having trouble uploading your ID, make sure the photo isn't blurry, the ID is fully visible in frame & is in a well-lit area. Also ID must be a drivers license, passport, ID card or resident permit. The website prompts you on how to correctly upload your ID. Thanks, Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0603063204092425270804] Hi Bonita,
As a licensed lender, Transform Credit Inc. DBA Together Loans is registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), license number 1905055. We undergo annual audits to maintain compliance with state and federal regulations, ensuring we adhere to all applicable laws and standards.
We completely understand how frustrating this can be and appreciate you both taking the time to complete the process.

To clarify, your cosigner must upload their ID via the link sent to continue. We do this to prevent identity fraud from being committed against them. We require the physical ID for your cosigner to pass verification because our system is designed to ensure security and prevent identity fraud, which helps protect both borrowers and cosigners. We cannot verify their ID with pictures or scanned copy of it.

We accept Driver’s Licenses, Passports, ID cards & residence permits issued by the US Government.

Here are some key tips to ensure the ID is accepted:

- Make sure the photo is clear, fully visible in the frame, and taken in a well-lit area.
- Use the physical ID itself rather than a scanned or photocopied version.
- The instructions provided during the upload process, including the video verification should be followed by your cosigner only. 

If your cosigner is still experiencing issues, it may be helpful to try using a different device or browser when uploading the ID. They can use any trustable person’s phone too if your cosigner’s phone is unable to take clear picture. Ask them to use the link provided here to resubmit: https://tcrdt.com/s/61CCKBAV

We understand this process can sometimes feel overwhelming, especially when technology doesn’t cooperate. Please let your cosigner know that this step is vital to ensure everything is processed securely and accurately. If you or your cosigner need further assistance or have questions, feel free to reply to this message, and I’ll be happy to help.

Thank you for your understanding and patience!

Best regards,
Together Loans

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0615031013060602020309] REF: Your Experian Dispute Answer CB00877385
Hi Elizabeth,
 
Thank you for your contact.

We can see here that you applied for a loan of $5000 via the Transform Credit website and during the application process, signed up to the CreditBuilder. 

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us as a Borrower, it is completely optional, giving you the opportunity to opt-in, or continue the loan application without setting up a CreditBuilder Account.

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

How are these payments reported to the Credit Reporting Agencies? - In order for us to be able to report the successful payments to the credit bureaus we reflect a loan in the user's credit file, which is held in escrow by a corporate guarantor, hence a Credit Builder user never receives the loan. When the account is canceled or ends this loan is then reported as successfully paid off and no money is owed to us.
 
We confirm that your CreditBuilder Account is already canceled, and will be reported and updated as a closed account, which was successfully paid off in your Credit File.
 
Please remember that you don’t owe us any money for this CreditBuilder account.
 
For any other information, or further support we will be happy to help you.
 
Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi Elizabeth, we received a dispute from you and we sent you an answer about your CreditBuilder account which you set up back in February 2023 when you applied for this loan of $5000. The CreditBuilder is already closed, reported as settled and you do not owe any for this. Please check your email as we sent you this confirmation. 
Transform CreditBuilder Team

interactions.body: Hi Elizabeth, thank you for your voicemail. Currently, you aren't eligible for a top-up loan because the loan must be at least one year old, have never been more than one payment behind, and have a balance below $6249.00; however, this balance varies by state.  By continuing to make all future payments on time and in full, you may qualify for one in the future. This option is available exclusively through our app at https://tglns.com/s/60KPELWZ where you can check your eligibility and manage your account. Best, Together Loans

[Affordability Confirmation] To Paul Davis,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Your Credit Commitments: $0

Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

Amber @ Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Requesting lower loan without a GT SMS) Hi Retrina, Transform Credit requires a cosigner, regardless of the requested loan amount. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you and that we should too, regardless of your credit score. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a very good credit history with no trouble paying bills in the past, and who can afford to make the monthly payments if you don't. Although they don't have to be a homeowner, it may increase the possibility of us accepting them as your cosigner if they do own their own home. If you need to update your loan amount or term, you can do so here: https://tcrdt.com/s/MXXZ4GP7 Thanks, TransformCredit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Jason Hawkins,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

interactions.body:
(Appeals Auto Response BRW Email) [RE:Appeal] Hi Jason, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/NZ8QIFC7). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one TransformCredit loan at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $4500 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/NZ8QIFC7 and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://transformcredit.com/faq.

Jade@TransformCredit 

P.S. I am ready to pay $4500 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0628050308081400321308] We won't be able to help you. Hope you can find another lender who can.

interactions.body:
(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Help me please] Hi Jason, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/ORFB1VH8

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0628050308081400321308] Your account has been permanently closed and we are unable to reopen it or accept any new applications from you.

We appreciate your position and regret that this is our final decision. We won’t be able to assist further, and we strongly encourage you to seek alternative funding options elsewhere.

Thanks, TransformCredit

Hi Jason, 
We are sorry that we won't be able to help you.
We hope that you can find another lender to help you.
Thanks, 
Transform Credit

Hi Jason,

Thank you for your message, and for taking the time to explain your situation in detail. We understand that you are frustrated and disappointed by the outcome of your application and the impact this has had on your plans. Please know that we genuinely empathize with your position and appreciate your respectful tone and transparency.

At Transform Credit, we take the integrity of our application process extremely seriously. As a regulated lender, we are legally obligated to protect our customers—and ourselves—against fraud and misuse. During our standard pre-funding checks, we identified fraudulent information associated with the co-signer on your original application. Due to the severity of this finding, we were required to decline the application These procedures are in place not only to prevent fraud but also to ensure that every party involved in the loan enters into the agreement knowingly, lawfully, and responsibly.

We fully recognize that you may not have been aware of the fraudulent activity committed by your original co-signer. However, we must base our decisions on the facts uncovered during our verification process. We do not take these decisions lightly, and they are made to comply with legal standards and protect all involved parties.

While the restriction on your account must remain in place at this time, we do not question your personal integrity or intent. Should our policies or procedures change in the future, we will be sure to consider any follow-up communication made in good faith.

Thanks,
Transform Credit

Hi Jason,

As mentioned in our earlier message, we totally understand your concern.

But, while the restriction on your account must remain in place at this time, we do not question your personal integrity or intent. Should our policies or procedures change in the future, we will be sure to consider any follow-up communication made in good faith.

Hope you will find another lender to help you at this point in time.

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Kyle Schnider,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

interactions.body: (AI BW GTQs - GT requirements SMS) Hi Steve, a cosigner is someone who trusts you and agrees to make any repayments you don't. Most of our customers ask a friend or family member. Suitable cosigners have a credit score of 750+ and a history of paying bills on time. Homeowners don't usually need a score as high as 750 and anyone with over a 700 score has a fair chance of being accepted. If you're unsure if your cosigner will be accepted, ask them to complete their application online, and they will be informed if they are accepted. [Affordability Confirmation] To Deaudrey Pryor,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Deaudrey Pryor
IP: 23.241.254.106
DateTime (UTC): 08-06-2025
Stated Monthly Income: Benefits $2700
Rent: $750
Bills: $150
Other Expenses: $500
Your Credit Commitments: $399
Transform Credit Repayment: $197.900000
To be safe: $50
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $653.100000


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

We also have answers to our frequently asked questions online: https://transformcredit.com/Help/Contact. If you still have questions, you can reply to this sms explaining in detail and we'll get back to you asap. Jade, Transform Credit

(Borrower GT Default SMS) Hi Deaudrey, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/59OWUTOZ - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

(Borrower Gt Declined SMS) Hi, Deaudrey. Unfortunately, your cosigner was declined because they failed to meet the criteria in our underwriting checks. We can still get this loan paid out to you. We just need you to find a suitable cosigner. If you are not able to find another cosigner, you can respond with CANCEL to close your application. You can re-apply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com - Transform Credit

(Want to speak to someone - PreVC BRW SMS) Hi Deaudrey. If you need help, we have a 24/7 interactive call handler, +1 470 435 6300. This will give you live updates about your application! Calling allows us to give you instant updates avoiding long waits to speak to a person. We also have answers to our frequently asked questions online: https://transformcredit.com/Help/Contact. If you still have questions, you can reply to this sms explaining in detail and we'll get back to you asap. Jade, Transform Credit

(Borrower CreditBuilder - CANCEL Email) [RE:A message from Transform Credit] Hi Deaudrey,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that on 08/06/2025 you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $5000 loan. I can confirm that I have canceled your Transform CreditBuilder, you will not be charged again.

The transform credit builder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 700+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. 

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
James, TransformCredit

However, this does not mean that you have been declined, and we can still get this loan paid out to you. We just need you to find a suitable cosigner, with strong credit and a history of making repayments on time. (AI BW GTQs - GT requirements Email) [RE: Need help finding a cosigner?] Hi Deaudrey,

A cosigner should be someone who trusts you and agrees to make any repayments you don't. Most of our customers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner.

Suitable cosigners should have a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners don't usually need to have a score as high as 750 and are more likely to be accepted with a lower score, as long as they pay their bills on time. Generally anyone with over a 700 score has a fair chance of being accepted as long as they have a good payment history.

If you are unsure whether your cosigner will be accepted, we recommend asking them to complete their application online, where they will be informed if they are accepted or not. If you need to provide a new cosigner, please use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/59OWUTOZ

Thanks,
Transform Credit

interactions.body: Hi Joseorlando. Thank you for reaching out. We understand this situation has been disappointing. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. However, I can confirm that at this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages. Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into.  We appreciate your understanding. We can still pay out your $5000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/LVCWRUEQ Thanks, Transform Credit

interactions.body: [kenishiacotner] CA64ef2098e535250ee06e1fbd604e9b0a

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 030602CARE] Hi Alazha, 


The loan you took was $5000.00 over 48 months, with monthly payments of $197.90 and a total repayable of $9499.20. Your current balance of $5024.38 reflects the principal plus any interest accumulated to date since the loan was funded, as we do not charge future interest. Interest is accrued daily against the balance but charged monthly; if you make extra payments or even settle before the full term, you'll only pay interest for the time you had the loan.  

We would be unable to settle for lower than this amount as this is both you and Alexis contractual agreement a very short time ago. Additionally, since we do not charge future interest, the balance at this time is a settlement offer as it has only accrued interest to this date. Meaning the sooner you settle the loan the more you save in interest accrued. 

Additionally Missing payments or changing the due date will increase the balance by accruing extra interest. 


Thank you for informing us that you are considering working with a Debt Management Company or Attorney to help manage your debts. For absolute clarity, we would like to inform you of a few key points so that you can make an informed decision regarding the management of your loan.

When you decided to take out a loan with us, both you and Alexis agreed to be responsible for its repayment. This liability remains, regardless of any third-party involvement.

If you make payments through a Debt Management Company, those funds will be credited to your account. However, if there's a shortfall, or if payments stop altogether, Alexis, will be asked to cover any amount due immediately in line with their separate contractual agreement they have with us. Further, whilst we will cease contact with you directly if you decide to pursue working with a Debt Management Company/Attorney, this does not apply to your co-signer.

Please also be aware that should the loan remain past due, it will be reported as delinquent to both you and Alexis's credit file, negatively impacting your scores. We would then look to pursue legal action against you both, to reclaim the owed amount. This could involve measures such as garnishment of funds or a lien on any property you or Alexis owns.

At this point, as we have not received any official confirmation that you are being represented on this debt, with the relevant contact information, we'll continue our direct communications with you regarding the status of your loan.

We understand that managing debt can sometimes be stressful, but we feel it is important that you are aware of the consequences of the loan falling behind for all parties on the loan. If you need any further help, please get in touch and we would be happy to support you as best we can to avoid any escalation of this debt.

Thanks, 

Berginie Botero
Head of Collections at Transform Credit
Hi Alazha, I am sorry we missed your call. I have sent an email to you addressing your concerns. Thank you, Kenishia@TransformCredit
